text.title: Lightweight Persona Building Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Failure modes specific to single-pass persona generation: stereotypes, user-story confusion, segment collapse, and template drift.

antipattern.description: Treating AI output as ground truth when no real user data was supplied.
antipattern.reason: Without data, the LLM produces a plausible-sounding stereotype. If the team treats it as a real persona, design decisions are made on fiction. The [INFERRED] tags are the only protection — and they only work if the team reads them.

antipattern.description: LLM produces a user story format instead of a persona card.
antipattern.reason: LLMs conflate "persona" with "user story" when not explicitly constrained. Explicitly forbid story format in the prompt ("Output a persona card, not a user story").

antipattern.description: Sonnet collapses two distinct segments into one when the input description is ambiguous.
antipattern.reason: Similar vocabulary in the input description causes the agent to merge segments. Require the agent to state the assumed cluster boundary explicitly before producing the card.

antipattern.description: Haiku formatting agent receives no explicit template structure and produces inconsistent output across runs.
antipattern.reason: Haiku is purely mechanical — it cannot infer the team's preferred template. Without the exact template structure in the prompt, output format varies every run and creates downstream formatting work.

antipattern.description: Asking the agent to update an existing persona (add a field) without passing the full existing persona JSON.
antipattern.reason: Without the prior persona as context, the agent rewrites rather than extends. Pass the full existing persona JSON every time an incremental update is needed.

antipattern.description: Skipping human review under time pressure because the pipeline is fast.
antipattern.reason: Human review is the only validation gate in this thin pipeline. The speed advantage of single-pass generation is only valuable if the output is correct — AI errors here propagate directly into design decisions.
